summary: M16.4: route single-output W4 verifier projections (rows in {2,4,8}) from the WMMA awq_fusedw4_prefill_fp16/strided small-batch kernel to the byte-exact output-column-tiled GEMV (default-on). Exact AR preserved; verifier kernel time -5.6% at decode-tokens=8 (17.02 -> 16.07 ms/pass) and -10.7% at decode-tokens=4 (13.60 -> 12.15).

change.site: project_pack8_fp16 branch B2/C2 (single-output W4: o_proj/v_proj/out_proj etc.) now yields the rows in {2,4,8} verifier batch to the already-wired output-tiled B3/C3 path instead of awq_fusedw4_prefill_*
change.kernel_before: awq_fusedw4_prefill_fp16 / awq_fusedw4_prefill_strided_fp16 (WMMA small-batch prefill; tiles starve at tokens=4)
change.kernel_after: gemv_awq_pack8_output_tiled_fp16 / gemv_awq_pack8_output_tiled_transposed_fp16 (weight read once, output columns tiled across the B+1 rows)
change.bit_exact_test: tests/test_paro_awq_output_tiled_gemv.py (192 cases: output-tiled == per-row strided/transposed pack8 GEMV, byte-identical, rows {2,4,8}, fp16/bf16). The per-row GEMV is AR's rows==1 projection, so the verifier rows become byte-exact vs AR -> exact-AR cannot regress.

correctness.note: Output-tiled is bit-identical to the per-row pack8 GEMV (the exact rows==1 AR fallback), so no prompt can change verifier output at these sites.

notes: First measured M16 gain (M16.1/M16.2 were diagnostics that closed graphs + native-loop). Only ~half of single-prefill (the rows in {2,4,8} project_pack8 sites) converts; the remaining single-prefill and the w4_dual_prefill_smallbatch (30 calls/0.92 ms/pass) duals are the next M16.4 step (dual output-tiled kernel is also byte-exact-tested). Pass composition differs by decode-tokens, so compare baseline-vs-exp within a single decode-tokens setting only.
